message:
fix(vacuum): evaluate WHERE clause for partial indexes during persist

The VACUUM persist step was inserting ALL table rows into every index
B-tree, ignoring partial index WHERE clauses. This caused rows that
don't match the predicate to appear in the index (e.g., a row with
assignee=NULL in an index with WHERE assignee IS NOT NULL).

Fix: parse the index's where_clause string into an Expr, then evaluate
it against each row during the B-tree population loop. Rows that don't
satisfy the predicate are skipped. If parsing or evaluation fails, the
row is included as a safe default.

Made eval_join_expr and is_sqlite_truthy pub(crate) so the persist
module can reuse the same expression evaluator as integrity_check.

Verified with beads_rust's full schema: br doctor now reports
OK sqlite.integrity_check (was ERROR for idx_issues_assignee).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
